help.title: Philosophy & Fabric Care Guide
help.subtitle: Learn to build outfits, calculate wear, and care for your garments.
help.philosophyP1: Armario Studio moves away from fast, disposable fashion toward an organized, functional experience. The goal is to combine pieces you already own, tracking wears to discover your personal style and reduce waste.
help.cpwDesc: Every time you wear a garment, you log a wear. The most worn pieces are your wardrobe stars. The least worn invite you to try new combinations.
help.fabric1Care: Hand wash or delicate cycle in cold water. Line dry in shade. Never tumble dry.
help.fabric2Care: Air out after wear. Wash inside out in cold bath at most every 6 months to preserve color.
help.fabric3Care: Hand wash with wool wash. Always dry flat on a clean towel.
help.fabric4Care: Wipe clean with damp microfiber after wear. Condition monthly with leather balsam.

audit.title: Audit Panel
audit.subtitle: Enterprise product status: quality, UX/UI, SEO, accessibility, performance, security and scalability.
audit.descBackend: Local persistence via IndexedDB works, but lacks validation layer, normalization, schema versioning and data contracts for production.
audit.descFrontend: Good use of code-splitting (lazy/Suspense) and reusable components. Main gap: modal/semantic accessibility and error handling.
audit.descDatabase: IndexedDB is correct, but missing migration strategies by version, indexes, and a unified read/write pattern.
audit.descResponsive: Top/bottom navbar and adaptive grids. For WCAG/UX, focus states consistency and minimum touch targets are needed.
audit.descSecurity: Auth is demo (local hash) and admin is UI-controlled. Production requires real backend, roles/permissions and XSS/CSRF mitigation.
audit.descArchitecture: Logic and state centralized in App.tsx without service/validation layer. UI-state modularization (modals/toasts/errors) is missing.
audit.descScalability: No caching, no pagination strategy for large lists and no formal data model. Enterprise needs repository layer and memoized selectors.
audit.descDocumentation: README exists but lacks technical documentation: design decisions, accessibility guide, data contracts and release checklists.
audit.descOptimization: View lazy-loading and controlled animations. Extra audit needed: images (sizes/srcset), charts memoization and Reduce Motion handling.
audit.descConversion: UX is value-oriented (wardrobe/outfit-building). For CRO, microcopy, measurable CTAs, onboarding and event tracking are needed.
audit.descUX: Clear flow by sections, consistent visual hierarchy. For enterprise: full accessibility (keyboard/ARIA) and form feedback improvements.
audit.descSEO: SPA without SSR: insufficient route-level indexability and metadata for enterprise plan. Pre-render/SSR and semantic structure per view required.
audit.moduleAccessibilityDesc: Fix modals and control semantics, add focus trap, aria-modal and keyboard support.
audit.moduleSecurityDesc: Replace demo auth with real backend, roles/permissions and endpoint protection. Replace confirm() with accessible modals.
audit.moduleSEOPerformanceDesc: Implement SSR/pre-render + optimize images (srcset/sizes) and reduce chart render cost.
audit.moduleTestingDesc: Add test suite (unit + e2e), CI/CD pipeline and performance checklist to guarantee target Lighthouse score.
audit.note: Note: this panel is designed to act as the audit "single source of truth". The next iteration will populate Roadmap + weekly/monthly dashboards + task management.
